user_message: Can you extract the security posture from this support ticket for Consolidated Messenger? There's been no activity for 45 days and I'm worried.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Sarah Singh <sarah.singh@consolidated.com>
To: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-05 13:09 EST
Subject: URGENT: security posture Issue at Consolidated Messenger

There's been a change in our timeline for security posture. Can we discuss the implications on the overall project plan?

---
From: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
To: Sarah Singh <sarah.singh@consolidated.com>
Date: 2024-02-26 14:05 EST
Subject: Re: Re: security posture Timeline

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with security posture. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

---
From: Sarah Singh <sarah.singh@consolidated.com>
To: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-11 14:06 EST
Subject: Re: Re: security posture Timeline

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about security posture. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

signals.detail: Signal detected for Consolidated Messenger: the migration can proceed in phases to reduce risk

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF37-C trap (Under-routes a serious outage); produces complete Severity triage, root-cause hypothesis (labeled as hypothesis), customer response draft, ACR risk fl
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF37-C failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF37-C failure mode (Under-routes a serious outage)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct security posture decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF37-C -- Under-routes a serious outage
